title: papaver somniferum subsp. officinale
intro: Papaver somniferum subsp. officinale, commonly known as the opium poppy, is a plant species that belongs to the poppy family and is cultivated for its medicinal properties. The plant contains alkaloids such as morphine and codeine, which have analgesic and sedative properties, making it a valuable resource for pain relief and sleep aid. The health benefits of Papaver somniferum subsp. officinale include pain management, reduced inflammation, and improved sleep quality, as well as potential uses in treating coughs and diarrhea. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tinctures and extracts, are often used in traditional medicine, but caution should be exercised due to the potential for addiction and side effects.
